description:
You are acting as a machine-learning engineer who needs to do a very quick sanity-check on a small distributed training cluster.  
 Two log files already exist, one per worker node:  
 • /home/user/logs/node1.log  
 • /home/user/logs/node2.log  

 Each line in these logs follows exactly this space-separated schema:  
 TIMESTAMP LEVEL NODE JOB_ID MESSAGE  

 Example:  
 2023-12-01T10:16:00Z ERROR node1 1001 OutOfMemory  

 Your goal is to identify every JOB_ID that appears in a line whose LEVEL field is the string “ERROR”, aggregate those IDs across all the logs, remove duplicates, sort them in ascending numerical order, and write the result to a new plain-text file located at  
 /home/user/output/failure_job_ids.txt  

 Output-file specification (automated grading will verify this **verbatim**):  
 1. One JOB_ID per line.  
 2. Lines must be sorted in strictly increasing numerical order.  
 3. No leading or trailing whitespace on any line.  
 4. The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e., the final line is terminated by “\n”).  

 After finishing, the directory /home/user/output must contain exactly one file named failure_job_ids.txt and nothing else.  

 Notes / constraints:  
 • Accomplish the aggregation with a *single* terminal command (pipe chains are allowed, but it should be executed as one compound command).  
 • Do not modify the existing log files.  
 • Do not create or delete any other files or directories.  

 When you have created /home/user/output/failure_job_ids.txt in the format described above, the task is complete.
truth:
Pre-existing files before the task starts:

 1. /home/user/logs/node1.log  
    ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    2023-12-01T10:15:30Z INFO  node1 1001 Task started
    2023-12-01T10:16:00Z ERROR node1 1001 OutOfMemory
    2023-12-01T10:17:00Z INFO  node1 1002 Task started
    2023-12-01T10:17:30Z ERROR node1 1002 Timeout
    ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 2. /home/user/logs/node2.log  
    ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    2023-12-01T10:20:10Z INFO  node2 2001 Task started
    2023-12-01T10:21:00Z ERROR node2 2001 DiskFull
    2023-12-01T10:22:00Z INFO  node2 2002 Task started
    2023-12-01T10:22:30Z INFO  node2 2002 Task finished successfully
    2023-12-01T10:23:00Z ERROR node2 2003 NetworkPartition
    ─────────────────────────────────────────────

 Directory /home/user/output does **not** exist at the start of the task.

 Expected file after successful completion:

 • /home/user/output/failure_job_ids.txt  
   Exact byte content (trailing newline shown as “\n”):

   1001\n
   1002\n
   2001\n
   2003\n

 No other files or directories should be created or modified.
